english: compound tags-brán byéd-pa (thags bran byed pa) to weave Mil.; nye-brán (nye bran) Mil. seems to be some decoration of the shoes; sño-brán (sngo bran) Mil. something similar. — ču-brán (chu bran) Glr., and mtso-brán (mtsho bran) ??
to pour out Tar.
1. v. bran (bran) 1. extr. — 2. also = *ḍan-tsós* (dan tsos) W. finger, toe.
v. ₒbráb-pa (brab pa).

from 1. Brahmin, Hindoo priest; brám-ze-mo (bram ze mo) female Brahmin; brám-ze rig-byéd ₒdón-pai sgra (bram ze rig byed 'don pa'i sgra) the voice of a Brahmin reciting the Vedas, being taken as a sign of good luck; brám-ze-pa (bram ze pa) an adherent of Brahma. — 2. a priest in general S.O. (Acc. to Fouc. transl. of Gyatch. 13 and 52 also = brāhmaṇa, the theological part of the Vedas; this is however against the tenor of the Tibetan text, which requires the word to be taken in the former sense.)
v. ₒbrál-ba (bral ba).
v. ₒbrí-ba (bri ba).
1. Sch. 'to continue, to reiterate, to repeat continually'; bríd-la ɣtón-ba (brid la gton ba) to give again and again'. — 2. v. ₒbríd-pa (brid pa).
Sch. to float, to move confusedly, before one's eyes.
Lt.?
v. ₒbrím-pa (brim pa).

v. ₒbrí-ba (bri ba); bris-sku (bris sku), sku-bris (sku bris) picture of a saint, drawn or painted Cs. — bris-ₒbúr (bris 'bur) the art of painting and carving images. — bris-ma (bris ma) written book. — nag-bris (nag bris) a drawing Cs.; tson-bris (tshon bris) a coloured picture.
v. ₒbrú-ba (bru ba).
Lex. hunger.
Wdk., prob. = gru-žá (gru zha) and gru-šá (gru sha) Pth., ₒbru-šál (bru shal) or ₒbru-šál (bru shal) Ld.-Glr. Schl. 19, b. 21, a. name of a country to the west of Tibet, bordering on Persia.
to flow, to stream, to gush Cs.; sbst. current, flow, flux Cs.; ču brúg-pa (chu brug pa) flowing-water Lex.
dirt, dung, excrements, mi-brún (mi brun), bya-brún (bya brun), sbraṅ-brún (sbrang brun) etc. feces of men, birds, flies etc. Med. and elsewh.
v. ₒbrúb-pa (brub pa).

small particles, fritters, bits, crumbs, bag-brul (bag brul) C. crumbs of bread; brúl-ba (brul ba) Mil., C. to fall, into an abyss Thg.; to fall off, fall out, fall down, of leaves, seeds etc.; brúl-bu (brul bu), brul-lu (brul lu) = brut (brut) W.
v. ₒbrú-ba (bru ba).

, *ḍe* (de), 1. a measure for dry things as well fluids, about 4 pints; acc. to Cs. 1/20 of a ₒbo ('bo); bré-bo čè (bre bo che), breu čuṅ (bre'u chung) large and small bre (bre), Cs.; ɣsér-pýe bre gaṅ (gser phye bre gang) Glr. one (small) measure of gold-dust; bre-do (bre do) two measures; bré-la ɣšoṅ (bre la gshong) that will just fill a bre (bre) Zam.; bres bšar-ba (bres bshar ba) to measure with a bre (bre) Lex.; lha-kaṅ bre-tsad tsam žig (lha khang bre tshad tsam zhig) a miniature temple, not larger than a bre (bre) Glr.; vulgo also that part of the Chod-rten (mchod rten), which has the shape of a corn-measure; in a general sense, measure, bre-sraṅ ɣtán-la ₒbébs-pa (bre srang gtan la 'bebs pa) Glr. to regulate measures and weights. — 2. *bre* (bre) Ld. Lh. *bre-sé* (bre se) Kun. Eremurus spectabilis, a plant of about a man's height, belonging to the asphodels. — 3. v. bré-ba (bre ba).
basin for washing C.
medicinal herb; bré-gu (bre gu), id. (perh. the same plant) Med.
v. ₒbré-ba (bre ba); bla-bré (bla bre), ka-bré (ka bre) Sch. capital, chapiter, upper part of a column or pillar.
Sch. unfit, useless, worthless; bré-moi ɣtam (bre mo'i gtam) Thgy.
v. ₒbrég-pa (breg pa).
v. ₒbréṅ-ba (breng ba).

to be frightened, afraid, in fear = rtáb-pa (rtab pa), B. and C.; sbrúl-gyis dṅaṅs-šiṅ bréd-pa (sbrul gyis dngangs shing bred pa) to be frightened by a snake Wdṅ., or bréd-čiṅ dṅaṅs-pa (bred cing dngangs pa) Pth.;
